name: wilson-hexa-first-warn
description: PreToolUse guard on Write AND Bash when a new file in the protected set is about to land ‚Äî acts on the hexa-first principle, pointing the agent at `.hexa` as the primary replacement (`.hexa` absorbs scripting + firmware authoring in this project), or at hexa-lang upstream PR-only (`hexa atlas register <file>` ‚Üí `hexa atlas pr`) when no existing path fits. Protected extensions: scripting (`.py`, `.sh`) plus firmware (C: `.c` `.h` `.cpp` `.hpp` `.cc` `.hh`; assembly: `.s` `.S`; RTL: `.v` `.sv` `.vhd` `.vhdl`). General-app languages (.rs, .go, .java, .js, .ts, ‚Ä¶) stay out of scope to avoid noise. Two tool surfaces ‚Äî both routed to the same decision logic: `Write` (tool_input.file_path matches a protected ext) and `Bash`. Bash matcher has TWO anchored sub-patterns: (a) redirect (`>` / `>>`) or `tee [-a]` whose target ends in a protected ext (closes `cat > foo.py <<EOF` / `echo ‚Ä¶ > out.sh` / `tee log.c`); (b) `cp` / `mv` / `ln` / `install` / `rsync` / `touch` whose LAST positional arg ends in a protected ext (closes the documented residual: `cp tmpl dest.sh`, `mv tmp dispatch.sh`, `ln -s src link.sh`, `touch new.sh`). Both anchored on the DESTINATION token only ‚Äî source-as-protected (`cp script.sh dest/`) never triggers. Two modes, selected by SIDECAR_HEXA_FIRST_WARN_MODE (WILSON_ accepted): `block` (DEFAULT) ‚Äî the call is DENIED at PreToolUse via permissionDecision, emitting an AI-native single-line directive (HEXA_FIRST_BLOCK { trigger, target, ext, primary, extend, downgrade }); the model must write `.hexa` instead, extend upstream via PR, or explicitly downgrade. `warn` ‚Äî a single-line advisory (HEXA_FIRST_WARN { ... }) is injected via additionalContext and the call proceeds unchanged. Any value other than `warn` resolves to `block` ‚Äî block is the floor. New-file moment only (`Write` matcher, NOT `Edit`) ‚Äî editing an existing protected file does not nag every time. Known residual bypasses still uncovered: in-process Python `open('foo.sh','w')` driven from `python -c '‚Ä¶'` (the .ext string is inside the python script body, not the bash command surface) and `git checkout`/`git restore` resurrecting a deleted protected file. Implements hexa-first as enforcement complementing wilson-prefs's principle-text injection ‚Äî text says "prefer hexa", this stops at the file-creation moment. Opt out entirely: SIDECAR_NO_HEXA_FIRST_WARN=1 (or WILSON_NO_HEXA_FIRST_WARN=1).
